prerequisites: A Fastly account with access to the Fastly control panel or API.

A Fastly API token (the value supplied as fastly_api_token). Create it through the Fastly web interface or via the POST /tokens API endpoint. Tokens carry the same permission model as the user who creates them, so a token with sufficient scope (for example global or global:read) is needed to read the data you intend to sync.
A start_date value marking the earliest point from which to begin syncing data.

authMethods.name: API token (Fastly-Key)
authMethods.summary: Fastly authenticates API requests with an API token. The connector sends this token (the fastly_api_token field) in the Fastly-Key request header. Tokens can be scoped (global, global:read, purge_select, purge_all) and inherit the permissions of the user who created them. A token with read access, such as global or global:read, is appropriate for syncing. The token value is shown only once at creation and cannot be retrieved later, so store it securely.

setupSteps.title: Create a Fastly API token
setupSteps.body: Sign in to Fastly and create an API token, either through the Fastly web interface or by calling the POST /tokens API endpoint. Choose a scope that covers the data you want to read (for example global or the read-only global:read). If two-factor authentication is enabled on your account, include the one-time password via the Fastly-OTP header when creating the token through the API. Copy the token value immediately, as it cannot be retrieved again after creation.

setupSteps.title: Provide the API token to the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the token as the fastly_api_token field. The connector passes it in the Fastly-Key header on every request to authenticate with the Fastly API.

setupSteps.title: Set the start date
setupSteps.body: Enter a start_date to define the earliest point from which the connector begins syncing data. Records older than this date are not pulled on the initial sync.

sources.title: Fastly API reference (authentication and Fastly-Key header)
Fastly Documentation: User tokens (scopes and POST /tokens)
